summary.intent: Implement a Nostr DM channel for the Moltis messaging platform, adding NIP-04 encrypted direct message support with relay management, access control, and web UI configuration.
summary.outcome: Full Nostr channel implementation delivered across ~25 files and ~2,200 LOC: new `crates/nostr/` crate with 23 passing unit tests, gateway registration, config schema, web UI add/edit/onboarding flows, E2E Playwright tests, and documentation.

repo: Channel plugins follow a strict pattern: `ChannelPlugin` trait in `crates/channels/src/plugin.rs`, optional dep in gateway/httpd/cli with feature flags, named field in `ChannelsConfig` in `crates/config/src/schema.rs`, entry in `build_schema_map()` in `validate.rs`, and registration block under `#[cfg(feature = "...")]` in `crates/gateway/src/server.rs`.
`ChannelType::ALL` is used in a test that asserts the count of variants — adding a new variant requires updating the count in the channels crate test.
`moltis_channels::message_log::MessageLog` is not re-exported at the crate root; must import from the full module path.
`ChannelOtpProvider` and `OtpState` live in `moltis_channels::plugin` and are re-exported at crate root.
The onboarding flow (`onboarding-view.js`) has a separate `ChannelTypeSelector` and `ChannelStep` routing that must be updated independently from `page-channels.js` — easy to miss.
`channelDisplayLabel()` in `onboarding-view.js` maps channel type strings to display names and needs updating for new channel types.
Web JS uses biome for linting/formatting; run `biome check --write` from the web/ui directory after JS changes.

Config `ChannelsConfig` uses `#[serde(flatten)]` for unknown channel types via `extra: BTreeMap`; only explicitly named fields (telegram, whatsapp, etc.) get dedicated struct fields — matrix was NOT a named field before this PR.
The `channels.offered` default list in `schema.rs` controls which channels appear in the web UI picker; new channels must be added there.

code.finding: ChannelType::ALL constant and a test asserting its length both need updating when adding new variants.
ChannelsConfig has named fields for each channel type plus a serde(flatten) extra map. Adding a named field for matrix was reverted as an unintended side effect.
Uses wildcard arm in ChannelType match, so adding new variants does not require changes here.
nostr-sdk v0.44 client.notifications() returns a receiver that must be bound to a variable before use in a while-let loop to avoid temporary drop issues.
client.disconnect() returns () not Result in nostr-sdk v0.44; must not use ? operator on it.

workflow: nostr-sdk v0.44 does not enable `nip04` in default features — must explicitly add `features = ["nip04"]` in workspace dependency.
nostr-sdk's `ToBech32` trait (for `to_bech32()`) returns `Result<String, Infallible>` not `String` — callers must `.unwrap()` or use `.expect()`.
nostr-sdk v0.44 `client.subscribe()` takes a single `Filter`, not `Vec<Filter>`.
nostr-sdk `Metadata::picture()` takes a `Url` type, not `String` — must parse the URL first.
taplo fmt reorders TOML keys alphabetically across the workspace; cosmetic-only changes appear in unrelated crates' Cargo.toml files after running it.

friction: nostr-sdk v0.44 API surface required multiple compile-fix-recompile cycles to discover correct method signatures (subscribe, disconnect, notifications, Metadata::picture, ToBech32 return types).
The `nip04` feature not being in nostr-sdk defaults was a non-obvious blocker that only surfaced at compile time.
Adding `matrix` as a named field in ChannelsConfig was an unintended side effect that required a follow-up revert commit.
The onboarding flow in `onboarding-view.js` is a separate concern from `page-channels.js` and was initially missed, requiring a third commit.
open_items: NIP-44 outbound encryption: currently defaults to NIP-04 for broad compatibility; config option to prefer NIP-44 is deferred.
NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs: deferred — requires more complex event handling and has limited client support.
NIP-65 relay list advertisement: deferred future enhancement.
NIP-94 media attachments: deferred future enhancement.
Seen-tracker persistence to disk (`data_dir()/nostr_seen.json` with debounced writes) is stubbed but not implemented — currently in-memory only.
Circuit-breaker metrics for relay health are not yet wired to the moltis-metrics crate (the `metrics` feature in nostr/Cargo.toml is defined but not populated).

Contract test suite (`moltis_channels::contract`) not run against NostrPlugin — the contract test design requires a startable account with empty config, which conflicts with Nostr requiring a valid secret key.
